all.title: Attention Is All You Need
all.abstract: We propose a new network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely.

all.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
all.abstract: We introduce a new language representation model called BERT, which stands for Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers.

all.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
all.abstract: We demonstrate that scaling up language models greatly improves task-agnostic, few-shot performance, without any gradient updates.

all.title: EfficientNet: Rethinking Model Scaling for Convolutional Neural Networks
all.abstract: We propose a compound scaling method that uniformly scales depth, width, and resolution in a principled way.

all.title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale
all.abstract: We show that Vision Transformers outperform previous state-of-the-art models on image classification while requiring fewer computational resources.

all.title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision
all.abstract: We demonstrate that simple pre-training task of predicting which caption goes with which image is an efficient and scalable way to learn image representations.

all.title: Diffusion Models Beat GANs on Image Synthesis
all.abstract: We show that diffusion models can achieve image generation quality superior to state-of-the-art GANs while maintaining better diversity and coverage.

all.title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
all.abstract: We introduce LLaMA, a collection of foundation language models ranging from 7B to 65B parameters, trained on publicly available datasets.

all.title: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
all.abstract: We show that fine-tuning language models with human feedback enables much better instruction following behavior.

all.title: PaLM-E: An Embodied Multimodal Language Model
all.abstract: We present PaLM-E, a generalist language model for robotics that incorporates real-world continuous sensor modalities into language models.

all.title: Segment Anything
all.abstract: We introduce the Segment Anything Model (SAM), a promptable segmentation system with zero-shot generalization to diverse image domains.

all.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
all.abstract: We propose Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), a parameter-efficient fine-tuning method that frozen model weights and injects trainable rank decomposition matrices.

all.title: Self-Instruct: Aligning Language Model with Self Generated Instructions
all.abstract: We introduce Self-Instruct, a framework that leverages language model itself to generate instruction data for tuning.

all.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
all.abstract: We demonstrate that chain-of-thought prompting improves the ability of large language models to perform complex reasoning tasks.

all.title: GPT-4 Technical Report
all.abstract: We report the development of GPT-4, a large-scale, multimodal model capable of accepting image and text inputs and producing text outputs.

all.title: Stable Diffusion: High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models
all.abstract: We propose Latent Diffusion Models (LDM) that achieve new state-of-the-art results for image synthesis while significantly reducing computational requirements.

all.title: Prompt Engineering for Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
all.abstract: We provide a comprehensive study of prompt engineering techniques for text-to-image diffusion models.

all.title: Evaluating Large Language Models on Code Generation
all.abstract: We evaluate the ability of large language models to generate correct and efficient code across various programming tasks.

all.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
all.abstract: We introduce Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), a hybrid model that combines parametric memory with non-parametric memory for knowledge-intensive tasks.

all.title: Convolutional Neural Networks for Sentence Classification
all.abstract: We report on a series of experiments with convolutional neural networks (CNN) trained on top of pre-trained word vectors for sentence-level classification.
